name: render_dead_turn_liveness
description: QA-3 S8/A4 liveness set piece, constructed from the round-3 forensics chronology (IMG_2591, the '??' Aheli session 11:28): turn 1 submits and starts a tool that NEVER completes (dead turn — dropped terminal frame or a turn the gateway never finished), then turn 2 ('??') submits and streams. The render lane must self-heal turn 1 the moment turn 2's userMessage bounds the new current turn: turn 1 folds to a muted 'Interrupted' (non-streaming) row, turn 2 stays live — exactly ONE working surface, never the eternal double-working the owner captured. Silence between seq 3 and seq 4 is the dead window (in production the per-turn watchdog's stage-1 silent resync{last_seq} fires inside it; this fixture replays the deterministic render half — the state-machine stages are pinned by TurnLivenessTests).
recorded_by: HAND-AUTHORED to the wire format from docs/qa3-image-ledger.md IMG_2591 (provenance: forensics chronology; a dead turn is not deterministically recordable against the isolated gateway without fault injection)

submit.text: Yes, internal transfer is usually to rich

body.text: Yes, internal transfer is usually to rich

settled.note: turn 1 never settles on the wire — the invariant is the render lane's self-heal: exactly one streaming assistant row after seq 6, and turn 1's row interrupted + non-streaming.
